entry.title: MAD-HTLC
entry.summary:
In a recent correspondence, the complexities and innovations in blockchain technology were discussed with particular emphasis on a new analysis presented in a paper. The focus of this discussion was on the relationships between various elements such as Alice-fee, Bob-bribe, timelock durations, and the percentage of miner's hashpower. Notably, the paper takes an exploratory dive into reducing the timelock values to extremely low levels, a concept detailed in Algorithm 1 of the document. This approach pushes the boundaries of standard practices and ventures into what is described as 'reckless territory.'
Furthermore, the paper critiques the common assumption of a constant hash-rate distribution over time, highlighting its inadequacy especially when dealing with 'weak' miners over thousands of blocks. Such an assumption may hold in scenarios limited to the timelock of a Hashed Timelock Contract (HTLC), but it falls short in broader applications.
Additionally, the discussion touches on the operational dynamics within the Lightning Network, where both parties involved—referred to as Alice and Bob—require fidelity bonds. This requirement significantly increases the cost associated with channel lock-in, complicating transactions further. The scenario of miners potentially forming a cartel to achieve a 51% network control is also considered, underscoring a critical vulnerability in blockchain security.
These insights provide a deeper understanding of the intricate balance required in blockchain operations and development, particularly in enhancing security measures and optimizing transactional efficiency.
